title: The River Mother
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: After a catastrophic flood, a woman in a floating Amazon village discovers she is pregnant with a child conceived from the river itself.
long_description:
Yara has spent her life in Porto das Aguas, a floating village on the Amazon River, haunted by the disappearance of her mother who walked into the water one night and never returned. When the worst flood in living memory submerges the village, Yara is pulled into the depths and experiences a vision of the river's ancient consciousness. She emerges carrying a child she did not conceive — the river's first human daughter.

As the pregnancy accelerates at an unnatural pace, Yara seeks guidance from Dom Ceu, the village shaman, who reveals that her mother also carried the river's seed but chose to return to the water instead. The village turns against her. The river makes demands. And the child growing inside her is not entirely human.

Spanning the seasons of flood and drought, birth and transformation, The River Mother is a story of sacrifice, belonging, and the ancient covenant between humanity and the wild. Yara must choose between the daughter she loves and the river that needs her — and learn that some bonds are stronger than water.
main_characters: Yara — A woman of Porto das Aguas, chosen to carry the river's child
The River — The Amazon River as a living consciousness
Dom Ceu — The village shaman who guides Yara
Iara — Daughter of Yara and the river
Maria do Rosario — The village midwife
Joao — A fisherman whose fear turns to respect
Yara's Mother — A healer who became one with the river
